weather_report:
At mid-morning in Subiaco the sky looks scrubbed and uncomplicated, and the forecast holds it that way into early afternoon: clear, dry air, bright sun, and long, clean-edged shadows. It has the feel of a gentle winter day, cool in the shade but pleasantly warm on a sunlit bench; the UV will creep to a moderate bite around midday despite the season, so sunglasses and a touch of sunscreen are sensible. The light is crisp, the horizon well-defined, the sort of clarity that makes even an ordinary street seem newly drawn.

Light layers suit it best — a soft jumper over a tee, with a hat in your bag. It’s a day for unhurried chores and small satisfactions: walk to the grocer, let the washing breathe on the line, linger over a flat white at a table that catches the sun, deadhead a pot of geraniums, or pedal a short errand before the later afternoon cools. Keep plans simple and outdoors if you can; the weather is quietly on your side.
